target.name: cylib
target.founder: Lilian Schwich, Gideon Schwich, Paul Sabarny

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $200M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Graphyte
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M

private.name: Fourth Power
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $150M

private.name: Steady Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $110M

data.narrative:
Cylib is a Series A climate and energy company founded by Lilian Schwick, Gideon Schwick, and Paul Sabarny, having raised €140M+ to date including a €55M Series A, a €26.1M NRW grant, and a €63.4M BMWE grant. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable set of one public company (LNZA) and eight private peers in the climate and energy sector at similar stage.

The peer-median method, applied across three same-sector Series A companies—Graphyte ($200M), Fourth Power ($150M), and Steady Energy ($110M)—yields a median valuation of $150M, with a range spanning $110M to $200M. This represents the sole quantitative triangulation method supported by the provided data, directly anchoring the valuation estimate to observable market precedent.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $128M to $172M, centered on $150M, reflecting the peer median and modest downside and upside variance. The single-method limitation introduces material uncertainty: reliance on only three peers leaves the estimate vulnerable to outlier effects, and the small sample size precludes sensitivity analysis across revenue multiples, growth rates, or technology maturity. Stage consistency and sector focus strengthen the peer selection, but calibration against burn rate, capital efficiency, or product milestones is absent from available data. Further validation through revenue or cash-burn multiple analysis would improve confidence in the range.
